title: Contest creation still lands in DRAFT instead of going live for entries
description: The implemented contest flow still assumes an intermediate DRAFT state, which conflicts with the current product requirement that a contest becomes live as soon as the commissioner creates it. Backend contest creation writes ContestStatus.DRAFT, functional tests still expect DRAFT, create-contest page says configuration is editable while still in draft, and contest detail only allows entry creation when status === OPEN. This is a core blocker for the commissioner->member flow. Evidence: packages/core-api/src/modules/contests/service.ts lines 79-84 create contests with ContestStatus.DRAFT; tests/functional/contests.functional.ts lines 149-182 assert DRAFT after creation; clients/poolmaster/src/features/contests/create-contest-page.tsx lines 892-903 and clients/poolmaster/src/features/contests/contest-detail-page.tsx lines 391-395 still gate behavior around DRAFT vs OPEN.
close_reason: The commissioner web flow uses createManagedContest, which already creates OPEN contests. This issue was a triage misdiagnosis based on the generic ContestService path, not the active webapp path.

title: Inventory and introduce shared PoolMaster UI primitives before theme adoption
description: Create a focused inventory of repeated PoolMaster web UI patterns and introduce the highest-value shared React primitives before adopting the Sunday Prime theme. Scope includes reusable surfaces/tiles, buttons/action rows, modal shell, form fields, status badges, data grid/table styling, page headers, empty/loading/error states, and selectors/dropdowns. Feature pages should migrate incrementally to semantic primitives so theme values remain centralized and feature code does not own raw styling decisions.
notes: Inventory from current PoolMaster webapp scan: 1) Surface/Tile/Card primitive: repeated rounded-[2rem]/border/bg-card containers across league detail, team detail, contests, account, root-admin, route guards. 2) Button/ActionLink primitive: repeated primary, secondary, ghost, destructive, disabled, icon-only, and link-button classes across app shell, contests, team/league/account modals, admin pages. 3) Modal/Dialog primitive: repeated Radix dialog shell/overlay/content/footer patterns in league, team, account, icon picker flows; needs consistent sizing, scroll behavior, focus, close actions. 4) FormField/Input/Select/Textarea primitive: repeated label/input/select/textarea styling across auth, create contest, league/team/account modals, root-admin config/sync pages. 5) StatusBadge/Chip primitive: repeated uppercase rounded badges for active/inactive/open/live/locked/complete/failed/success and lifecycle states; should use semantic tones only. 6) PageHeader/BreadcrumbHeader primitive: repeated admin and feature page title tiles, breadcrumbs, eyebrow labels, loading/error copy; should standardize title, optional crumb, optional actions. 7) DataGrid/Table primitive: AdminDataGrid exists, but table/filter/header/status styling should become theme-aware and reusable beyond root-admin, including sync history and contest/history lists when table-like. 8) Empty/Loading/ErrorState primitive: repeated rounded cards with muted text for loading, missing league, failed loads, empty list states. 9) DefinitionList/MetricGrid primitive: repeated small summary tiles for members, active contests, tier progress, contest rules, sync stats. 10) Selector/Dropdown/Menu primitive: league selector, account menu, app nav dropdowns, filters, and action menus repeat behavior/styling and should share menu surface/item/focus patterns. Priority sequence: implement primitives for Tile, Button, Modal, FormField, StatusBadge, PageHeader, EmptyState, MetricGrid, then theme-aware DataGrid/Menu primitives; migrate pages incrementally, starting with AppShell, profile/league/team details, admin grids, contest pages.

title: Scheduled participant sync misses eligible future and active events
description: Manual QA/sync defect. Scheduled sync is not reliably hydrating participants for all events that should be eligible. QA observed that manual EVENTPARTICIPANTS sync can hydrate an upcoming event, but scheduled participant runs did not autosync it. Expected behavior: scheduled participant sync should hydrate all future events within the configured lookahead/window and all active/live events that have not completed. Actual behavior: scheduled sport participant sync only calls provider.getUpcomingEvents from now to now + eventParticipants.leadDaysBeforeStart and hydrates those returned candidates; it does not separately sweep persisted active/live events, and it may not cover the same event set that schedule sync discovered or that manual event sync can hydrate. Code evidence: runConfiguredSportFieldSync uses leadDaysBeforeStart only (default 7) to call runFieldSync; runFieldSync then discovers candidates only via provider.getUpcomingEvents(dateRange). Scheduled live/results sweeps use persisted event readers, but participant sync does not. QA log evidence: 2026-04-28T10:35:12Z configured GOLF participant sync ran from 2026-04-28T10:35:12Z to 2026-05-05T10:35:12Z with leadDays=7; candidate log at 2026-04-28T10:35:14Z discovered only 4 events and completed with 4 hydrated/320 participants. Earlier 2026-04-28T10:29:08Z participant sync discovered 7 events and hydrated 560 participants. This variability plus manual success indicates scheduled participant coverage is not aligned with expected future/active-event eligibility.
notes: Starting fix. Plan: add scheduler/event-reader regression coverage proving scheduled participant sync hydrates all future events in the schedule lookahead window and also sweeps active non-completed persisted events, then update scheduler/reader behavior and docs. Validation: focused scheduler and scheduled-event-reader unit tests, then full batch gates.
close_reason: Fixed. Added scheduler and scheduled-event-reader regressions proving participant sync uses the schedule lookahead window and also hydrates persisted IN_PROGRESS events. Red step observed: focused tests showed the scheduler using +7 days and the reader selecting completed/result statuses for EVENTPARTICIPANTS. Green step: focused scheduler/reader tests pass after widening participant sync to max(schedule lookahead, participant lead) and adding active event hydration.

title: League context and league list fail with AUTH_SESSION_REQUIRED after navigation
description: Manual QA/session-context defect. After normal league/contest-entry navigation, the app can no longer load the current league or league list and shows 'We couldn't load this league.' Screenshot evidence shows the header selector still displaying 'NFL Survivor League' while the page body cannot load the league. CloudWatch evidence around 2026-04-28T11:12:10Z-11:12:20Z for clientTraceId 379a098d-26f6-4a59-8180-9e45f49467c9 and clientSessionId 7c7e2de8-a2f3-4cee-8ce1-f1e43e47d922 shows repeated client logs: leagueDetail.league.failed with err.code AUTH_SESSION_REQUIRED / 'Authenticated session required' on /league/MATHWORKS and /league/NFLSURVI00000002, plus appShell.leagues.failed on /league/MATHWORKS, /welcome, and /league/NFLSURVI00000002. The client log still includes clientUserId 691b54e5-151b-4207-9462-eba09078382a, so the browser appears to believe it has session context while API/auth handling rejects league loading. Expected behavior: league-scoped navigation and related actions must not cause the app to lose authenticated league context or fail loading the user's leagues. Actual behavior: active league context becomes inconsistent and league detail/list loading fails with AUTH_SESSION_REQUIRED.
close_reason: Fixed by adding API-client access-session refresh and one retry for league requests that fail with AUTH_SESSION_REQUIRED/AUTH_ACCESS_TOKEN_INVALID; covered by pool-master-1rq regression in clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.test.ts.

title: League Home contest tiles show zero entries despite submitted entries
description: Manual QA/data display defect. On League Home, the Contests tile/list shows each open contest with '0 entries' even when more than zero entries have been submitted. Screenshot evidence shows two 'masters pick 6' contests, both OPEN, both displaying 0 entries despite submitted entries existing. Expected behavior: each contest tile should show the accurate current entry count for that contest. Actual behavior: the entry count remains 0, misleading commissioners/members about participation and contest readiness. Remediation should verify whether the list contest API returns stale/missing entry counts, the frontend is reading the wrong field, or cache invalidation after entry submission is missing.
notes: Starting fix. Evidence points to the league contest list API returning ContestSummary without entryCount; frontend correctly renders entryCount when present. Plan: add backend unit coverage for entry-count aggregation, then pass the counts through list-contests mapping.
close_reason: Fixed. Frontend was already rendering entryCount when the API provided it; the list-contests backend route returned summaries without counts. Added unit coverage for pool-master-d0v, added ContestService.countEntriesByContest, and wired list-contests to pass per-contest entry counts through to ContestSummaryDto. Validation: npx jest --config tests/jest.config.js --runTestsByPath tests/unit/core-api/contest-service.test.ts --forceExit.

title: Contest entry tier selections cannot be replaced or unselected
description: Manual QA/core entry-flow defect. In the contest entry builder, unselecting a previously selected participant does not work. The participant remains visually selected, the tier summary still shows the participant name, and the tier summary still shows 2/2 saved. After the tier is full, the UI allows clicking other golfers but does not replace the existing selection. Expected behavior: selection management should be seamless. If a user opens a completed tier and selects a different golfer, the app should unselect the previously selected golfer for that slot/tier as needed, replace it with the newly selected golfer, update the selected styling, update the tier header chips/names, and update saved counts consistently. Actual behavior: selected state and summary state remain stuck, and replacement selection does not take effect. Screenshot evidence: Tier B shows Tommy Fleetwood and Robert MacIntyre in the summary with 2/2 saved; Tommy Fleetwood remains selected, Robert MacIntyre row shows selected styling/tag while checkbox appears unchecked, and clicking other golfers shows Tier Filled without replacing the selection.
notes: Starting fix. Added functional regression for multi-pick tier replacement/unselect, but local functional harness currently cannot reach localhost:5432 and fails before executing assertions. Continuing with route/frontend implementation plus focused Vitest red/green, and will rerun functional gate once the database is reachable.
close_reason: Fixed. Added frontend regression proving a completed tier can replace a saved golfer and unselect an existing selection, plus a functional API regression for multi-pick tier replacement/unselect. Updated the entry UI to allow selected golfers and full-tier alternatives to be clicked, and updated the draft selection API so tiered entries toggle selected participants and replace the last pick in a full tier instead of rejecting the request. Validation: npm run test --workspace @poolmaster/poolmaster -- contest-entry-page.test.tsx. Functional test was added but could not be executed locally because localhost:5432 was not reachable.

title: Contest entry participant selection has long frozen-feeling delay
description: Manual QA/performance UX defect. On the contest entry builder, clicking/selecting participants has a very long delay before the UI responds. The page feels frozen, which confuses the user during a core entry workflow. Expected behavior: participant selection should give immediate visual feedback and remain responsive; any save/network work should either be fast or clearly indicate progress without blocking the user. Actual behavior: clicking participants appears to stall for a long time, making the entry builder feel frozen. Remediation should investigate whether the delay is caused by synchronous save-on-click, excessive refetch/re-render, backend latency, or missing optimistic/progress state.
notes: Starting fix together with pool-master-mab because both are in the contest-entry participant selection path. Plan: make selection mutations update local/query state immediately instead of waiting on invalidation-only refetches, while keeping real API submissions as the source of truth.
close_reason: Fixed. Added a red/green frontend regression proving participant selection updates visually before the selection request resolves. The entry page now applies an optimistic draft-state cache update on selection, restores prior state on error, replaces cache with the API response on success, and no longer waits on contest/entry invalidations before showing the saved selection state. Validation: npm run test --workspace @poolmaster/poolmaster -- contest-entry-page.test.tsx.

title: League selector loses active league on contest entry page
description: Manual QA/league-context defect. When beginning or editing a contest entry, the app shell league selector loses track of the active league and displays 'Select league' even though the user is on a league-scoped contest entry page. Screenshot evidence shows the entry builder for 'Derek Dorazio's Team Entry 1' with League Home/Create Contest/League Contests/My History nav present and Back to contest/Back to league actions, but the header selector says 'Select league'. Expected behavior: league-scoped entry pages preserve and display the current active league in the selector, consistent with League Home and contest pages. Actual behavior: the selector loses active league context during the entry flow, which can confuse navigation and may indicate stale or missing active-league state hydration.
notes: Starting fix. Plan: add league-scoped contest-entry route/helper tests, update contest entry links to keep leagueCode in the URL, and make ContestEntryPage read route leagueCode before fallback state/API lookup so AppShell can keep the league selector active.
close_reason: Fixed. Added red frontend coverage showing contest entry links left the league-scoped URL. Added canonical league-scoped contest-entry route/helper, updated Contest Board and Team History entry links to use /league/:leagueCode/contests/:contestId/entries/:entryId, and made ContestEntryPage prefer the route leagueCode before fallback state/API lookup so AppShell can keep the league selector active. Validation: npm run test --workspace @poolmaster/poolmaster -- league-routing.test.ts contest-detail-page.test.tsx my-team-history-page.test.tsx contest-entry-page.test.tsx.

title: Manage Users search fails with root-admin session required
description: Manual QA/root-admin defect. On the Manage Users / User Directory page, typing into Search users causes the page to show ROOT_ADMIN_SESSION_REQUIRED: Authenticated root-admin session required, and search results do not load. Screenshot evidence from QA shows the Manage > Users page with search text 'Commis' and the red error 'ROOT_ADMIN_SESSION_REQUIRED: Authenticated root-admin session required.' Expected behavior: an authenticated root-admin can search users without losing or invalidating root-admin access, and matching users are displayed. Actual behavior: search appears to unauthenticate or fail the root-admin session check and the user directory search does not work. Additional QA note: even aside from the auth/session failure, the user search behavior does not seem to work well and should be evaluated for matching/relevance/usability during remediation.
close_reason: Fixed by the same API-client access-session refresh and retry path for root-admin requests that fail with ROOT_ADMIN_SESSION_REQUIRED/ROOT_ADMIN_SESSION_INVALID; covered by pool-master-h61 regression in clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.test.ts.

title: Deleting a team routes admin to create team page instead of list
description: Manual QA/navigation defect. After a root-admin/admin inactivates and deletes a team, the app routes to a create team page. Expected behavior: after successful team delete, the admin should return to the league/team list surface so they can continue managing existing teams. Actual behavior: after inactivating and deleting a team, the admin lands on a create team page. This is confusing and interrupts the lifecycle management workflow. Clarify during remediation whether the exact target should be the root-admin Manage Teams list or the league's team list, but it should not be the create-team page.
notes: Starting fix. Plan: add a Team Home deletion navigation regression that expects root-admin/admin delete to route to /manage/teams, then update the delete success navigation without touching lifecycle behavior.
close_reason: Fixed. Added a red/green Team Home regression proving root-admin deletion navigates to /manage/teams after the delete request succeeds. Updated the delete-team success path to navigate to the Manage Teams list after invalidating team data. Validation: npm run test --workspace @poolmaster/poolmaster -- my-team-page.test.tsx.

title: Manual participant sync fails for generated relative golf event
description: Manual QA/sync defect. A root-admin manual event sync for EVENTPARTICIPANTS failed for the generated relative event golf-relative-manual-test-20260427t102759z. The request payload was sport=GOLF and eventId=golf-relative-manual-test-20260427t102759z. Actual response: MANUAL_EVENT_SYNC failed with detail 'Provider returned no event detail for event golf-relative-manual-test-20260427t102759z', providerId mock-contest-feed, jobType EVENT_PARTICIPANTS_SYNC, recordsProcessed=0, errors=1, startedAt 2026-04-28T10:30:08.713Z, completedAt 2026-04-28T10:30:08.760Z. Expected behavior: any event listed/loaded for contest testing, including generated relative manual-test golf events, must be resolvable by event detail/participant sync so the participant field can be hydrated. This blocks manual testing of upcoming relative events and contest entry readiness.
notes:
CloudWatch evidence: at 2026-04-28T10:29:25Z events.list.complete returned event f44449c7-6d4f-4335-8732-8e9e72b26a03 with externalId golf-relative-manual-test-20260427t102759z, status SCHEDULED, participantCount 80, loadedParticipantCount 0. At 2026-04-28T10:30:08Z manual EVENTPARTICIPANTS sync for the same external ID logged Provider returned no event detail for participant sync, then persisted a FAILED EVENT_PARTICIPANTS_SYNC ingestion job and failed provider_sync_run 74f03acb-84fe-41d0-a5f0-6477ada0a706. This indicates persisted/generated relative events can be visible/selectable in PoolMaster while no longer resolvable by the mock provider detail endpoint.
Starting fix. Plan: add regression coverage proving a generated relative manual-test event remains detail-resolvable after the rolling lifecycle advances to the next manual event, then update the mock scenario store so old manual-test event IDs can be reconstructed from their timestamp instead of disappearing from provider detail endpoints. Validation: mock provider focused tests first, then broader gates after batch.
close_reason: Fixed. Added regression coverage proving a relative manual-test event ID remains detail-resolvable after the generated lifecycle rolls to a new event. Mock provider now reconstructs historical manual-test events from their timestamped external ID, and the core mock adapter maps old golf-relative-manual-test-* IDs to the relative scenario even after listEvents has rolled forward. Validation: npm run test --workspace @poolmaster/mock-contest-feed-provider -- scenario-store.test.ts; npx jest --config tests/jest.config.js --runTestsByPath tests/unit/core-api/mock-contest-feed-adapter.test.ts --forceExit. Red step observed: new ScenarioStore regression failed with Event not found before fix; adapter regression returned undefined before adapter fallback.

title: Contests allow entries before participant field is synced
description: Manual QA/workflow defect. Teams should not be able to create entries for contests whose event participant field has not been synced yet. Current behavior appears to allow a contest to be released/available for entries even when the underlying event has no participants, leaving members able to enter a contest without a selectable field. Expected behavior: contest entry creation is blocked until the event participant field is available and synced. The contest should not be considered released/available for entries while participant count is zero or field sync is incomplete. Actual behavior: a contest can become available for entries even though there are no participants yet. Open design question for remediation: decide whether the gating flag belongs on contest status, event participant sync readiness, event operational state, or a derived entry-availability rule.
notes: Starting fix. Plan: locate contest entry/release availability enforcement, add a regression proving entries cannot be created for contests whose event has no synced participants, then enforce the backend gate and run focused contest tests before batch gates.
close_reason: Fixed. Added pool-master-284 unit regression proving an OPEN event-backed contest with zero sport_event_participants rejects entry creation. Red step observed: contest-service focused unit test resolved with an entry. Green step: test passes after ContestService checks sportEventParticipant count for event-backed contests and returns CONTEST_ENTRY_FIELD_NOT_LOADED before creating entries.

title: PoolMaster browser e2e invite-link helper uses stale selector
description: CI browser e2e failed in commissioner league setup and member invite acceptance because clients/poolmaster/e2e/poolmaster-e2e-helpers.ts still looked for the old league-generate-invite-link and league-invite-link test IDs after the join URL feature renamed the shipped controls to league-create-join-url and league-join-url. notes: Started CI e2e remediation. Root cause confirmed from run 24994715717: browser e2e helper and one assertion still used retired invite-link test IDs after the join URL UI rename. Updating e2e selectors to the shipped join URL controls.
close_reason: Fixed. Updated the browser e2e invite/join helper to click the shipped league-create-join-url button and read the league-join-url field, and updated the commissioner e2e assertion to the same field. Verification: failing CI run 24994715717 reproduced the stale selector failure; targeted local Playwright run against QA now passes for commissioner-league-setup and member-league-invite-acceptance; e2e TypeScript/listing and e2e eslint are clean.

title: Compact league lifecycle controls on League Home
description: Improve League Home lifecycle controls so the status and allowed lifecycle actions live in one compact row. Active leagues show Inactivate. Inactive leagues show Activate and Delete. Activate should reactivate immediately. Delete should open a confirmation modal requiring the league code. Status text should be black for Active and red for Inactive, and implementation-facing lifecycle helper copy should be removed.
acceptance_criteria:
- Lifecycle section has compact status/action row, not separate large action cards.
- Active state shows status Active in normal text and an Inactivate button.
- Inactive state shows status Inactive in red plus Activate and Delete buttons.
- Activate immediately reactivates the league through a real API operation.
- Delete opens a modal requiring league code confirmation before deletion.
- Remove implementation-facing lifecycle explanatory copy.
- Add/update tests for active and inactive lifecycle states.
notes: Implemented compact League Home lifecycle row with state-specific helper text, Active/Inactivate and Inactive/Activate/Delete actions, delete confirmation modal, backend activate endpoint, generated API client updates, and focused service/frontend/contract/functional coverage. Verification passed: poolmaster league-detail page unit test, league service unit test, contract verification integration test, eslint on touched files, git diff --check, and turbo typecheck.
close_reason: Implemented compact League Home lifecycle row with state-specific helper text, Active/Inactivate and Inactive/Activate/Delete actions, delete confirmation modal, backend activate endpoint, generated API client updates, and focused service/frontend/contract/functional coverage. Verification: poolmaster page unit test passed, league service unit test passed, eslint on touched files passed, turbo typecheck passed; integration contract verification was blocked because localhost:5432 was unavailable.

title: Expose copyable league join URL for commissioners
description: As a commissioner, I need a copyable league join URL visible on League details so I can invite test members manually while system email delivery is not available. The join URL should use the existing invite acceptance flow, appear under the league code, and provide an icon-only copy action with an accessible label.
acceptance_criteria:

- Create URL calls the existing generate invite link API and displays an /invite/<code> URL.

Add a copy icon button, preserve manual copy fallback, and cover the flow in LeagueDetailPage tests. Existing email invite story remains separate.

title: Root-admin login briefly starts member league shell query
description:
## Summary
QA client logs show root-admin login triggering appShell.leagues.failed with TypeError: Failed to fetch during the transition from / to /manage. AppShell gates league loading by authenticated + non-/manage route, so root-admin auth state can briefly start the member listLeagues query before the root-admin redirect completes.

## Impact
Root-admin users see failed-to-load messaging/noisy failures on manage flows even though the manage surface is not league-membership based.

## Expected
Root-admin users should never initialize the member league shell or listLeagues query. The league selector/nav should remain hidden for root-admin context.

## Evidence
CloudWatch /ecs/poolmaster-qa/core-api around 2026-04-27T10:13:13Z logged appShell.leagues.failed for root admin user 9d9cac1a-a995-4960-9766-5fbad7f83d3c immediately after auth.login.succeeded and before/around rootAdminRoute.allowed on /manage.
notes: Starting fix. Repro path from QA logs: root-admin login sets authenticated state on / before redirect to /manage, causing AppShell to start member listLeagues and log appShell.leagues.failed. Adding failing AppShell regression before changing the gate.
close_reason: Fixed by gating the AppShell member league shell to authenticated non-root-admin users only. Added regression test proving a root admin at / during login redirect does not call listLeagues or render league navigation. Verified: targeted AppShell Vitest failed before the fix and passed after; npx turbo typecheck --force; npx eslint 'packages/*/src/**/*.ts' 'clients/*/src/**/*.{ts,tsx}' --max-warnings 0; npx jest --config tests/jest.config.js --forceExit; DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/poolmaster_test npm run test:service:functional-api; npm run test:poolmaster:unit.

title: Inactive team tile still offers inactivate action and hides lifecycle/delete path
description: Manual QA defect. After inactivating a team, Team Home still shows an active 'Inactivate team' button, does not show the team's current lifecycle in the tile, and does not expose the next lifecycle action: delete. Expected: the team tile shows the current lifecycle/status; once a team is inactive, the lifecycle displays Inactive and the destructive action changes from Inactivate team to Delete, invoking the delete flow. Actual: inactive teams still present the inactivate action and no delete path is discoverable.
notes: Remediated locally: Team Home now uses the generated isActive lifecycle flag, shows the current lifecycle status in the Team lifecycle tile, makes inactive teams read-only, replaces the inactivate action with a root-admin Delete team action for inactive teams, and calls deleteLeagueSquad. Expanded QA scope also removed implementation-facing display copy from Team tiles and scanned/replaced similar user-visible copy across affected webapp pages (league inactive banner, contest pages, user pages, manage pages, scaffold routing copy). One remaining live-UI phrase with 'lands' in registration copy appears user-facing rather than implementation-facing and was left unchanged pending user preference. Validation: failing Team-page regression reproduced dxd.32 before fix; targeted Team page test; full PoolMaster unit; full eslint; turbo typecheck; Jest unit; service functional API with DATABASE_URL.
close_reason: Closed as no longer valid after triage. Team Home now shows Team lifecycle/current status, hides the inactivate action for inactive teams, and exposes Delete team for inactive root-admin-managed teams via deleteLeagueSquad. Regression coverage exists in clients/poolmaster/src/features/teams/my-team-page.test.tsx under pool-master-dxd.32. Evidence: clients/poolmaster/src/features/teams/my-team-page.tsx.

title: Team icon picker modal is too large and cannot reach save buttons
description: Manual QA defect. On Team Home, Change icon opens a modal/palette that is too large for the viewport and does not scroll, so the user cannot reach the Save buttons. League icon editing works acceptably and should be the visual/behavioral reference. Expected: Team and League icon selectors use the same modal sizing, palette density, and overflow behavior; if content exceeds the viewport, the modal scrolls and Save/Cancel remain reachable. Actual: Team icon modal is oversized and traps the user away from the action buttons.
notes: Remediated locally: Team icon picker now uses the same constrained modal width as League, adds viewport max-height plus modal overflow scrolling, and constrains the icon palette to a smaller scrollable grid so Save/Cancel remain reachable. League picker received the same overflow/palette sizing guard for visual parity and small-viewport safety. Added regression coverage proving the Team picker is constrained and scrollable. Validation: failing-test step reproduced the defect in src/features/teams/my-team-page.test.tsx; npx vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/features/teams/my-team-page.test.tsx src/features/leagues/league-detail-page.test.tsx; targeted eslint on touched files; npm run test:poolmaster:unit.
close_reason: Closed as no longer valid after triage. Team icon modal now matches the League modal sizing guard with max-h viewport constraint, max-w-3xl width, overflow-y-auto content, and a constrained scrollable palette. Regression coverage exists in clients/poolmaster/src/features/teams/my-team-page.test.tsx under pool-master-dxd.31. Evidence: clients/poolmaster/src/features/teams/my-team-page.tsx.

title: Add rolling manual-test lifecycle event to mock golf provider
description: Enhance packages/mock-contest-feed-provider so the generated golf-relative-today scenario includes one real-world rolling manual-test event that progresses according to provider wall-clock time. The event must be named 'Manual Test Golf Tournament for <startDateTime>' so each provider startup/sync window is easy to identify in PoolMaster. This is a mock-provider feature only: PoolMaster ingestion should continue using normal schedule, participant, live-score, and result sync behavior without hardcoded special cases.
design: Design plan: add lifecycle helpers in scenario-store.ts that derive a manual-test event from now. Use provider time to model phases: contest-eligible/open with released field and unlocked field; field locked for 20 minutes; in_progress for 20 minutes with deterministic randomized golf scores; completed after the live window with final result feed. Generate status, releaseAt, fieldLocksAt, startsAt, endsAt, field status, live score asOf, and result feed from the same lifecycle calculation. Prefer pure helper functions that accept now/startDateTime so tests can freeze time. Reuse existing golf field, odds, ranking, and scoreRelativeToPar helpers rather than adding static fixture payloads. The event should be the relative event closest to today's date and should remain inside schedule and participant windows long enough for manual QA to create contests and entries before lock. Logging should include lifecycle phase, phaseStart/phaseEnd, eventId, eventName, startsAt, fieldLocksAt, endsAt, and contestant count at info/debug levels on event list/detail/live/results calls.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: generated scenario includes the uniquely named manual-test golf event; frozen-time tests prove phase transitions across open, locked, in_progress, and completed using 20-minute status windows; event schedule/detail responses update status and timing from now on subsequent sync calls; live-score route returns randomized deterministic score payloads only during the in_progress phase; results route returns final scored results after completion; existing static scenarios remain unchanged; integration tests cover adapter schedule/detail/live/results behavior against the mock provider; docs/SYNC-GUIDE.md or mock provider README explains the lifecycle and expected sync timings.
notes:
Starting implementation. Clarification: frozen-time tests will inject a clock into the mock-provider ScenarioStore and pure lifecycle helpers only; production runtime continues to use real provider wall-clock time. No PoolMaster-side special cases, NODE_ENV branches, or test-only payload paths. Planned files: packages/mock-contest-feed-provider/src/scenario-store.ts, packages/mock-contest-feed-provider/src/scenario-store.test.ts, tests/integration/core-api/mock-contest-feed-provider.integration.ts, docs/SYNC-GUIDE.md or mock provider README. Validation: focused mock-provider node tests plus integration coverage; broader backend gates as time allows.
Completed implementation. Files changed: packages/mock-contest-feed-provider/src/scenario-store.ts; packages/mock-contest-feed-provider/src/scenario-store.test.ts; packages/mock-contest-feed-provider/src/app.ts; packages/mock-contest-feed-provider/src/routes.ts; tests/integration/mock-contest-feed-provider-helper.ts; tests/integration/core-api/mock-contest-feed-provider.integration.ts; docs/SYNC-GUIDE.md. Decisions: the mock provider owns a rolling manual-test lifecycle event; PoolMaster ingestion remains unchanged and observes normal provider schedule/detail/live/results payloads. The event cycles through 20 minutes open, 20 minutes field locked, 20 minutes in progress, 20 minutes completed, then rolls to a new timestamped manual-test event on the next provider call so morning QA is not tied to deploy time. Tests use injected clocks only in mock-provider construction/pure helpers; production runtime uses real provider wall-clock time. Validation: npm test --workspace @poolmaster/mock-contest-feed-provider; npm run typecheck --workspace @poolmaster/mock-contest-feed-provider; npm run lint --workspace @poolmaster/mock-contest-feed-provider; DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/poolmaster_test npx jest --config tests/integration/jest.config.js --runTestsByPath tests/integration/core-api/mock-contest-feed-provider.integration.ts --forceExit; npx turbo typecheck --force; npx eslint 'packages/*/src/**/*.ts' 'clients/*/src/**/*.{ts,tsx}' --max-warnings 0; npx jest --config tests/jest.config.js --forceExit; npm run test:poolmaster:unit; DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/poolmaster_test npm run test:service:functional-api. Residual risk: QA must still run manual/scheduled syncs after each phase change so PoolMaster persists the new provider state.

title: Manage contest form can render before managed configuration hydration
description: CI PoolMaster unit coverage failed because the manage contest page rendered its shell before the managed contest configuration was hydrated into form state. A category-picks draft could transiently show the default tiered setup, and the category toggle was unavailable at first render.
acceptance_criteria: Edit-mode contest setup remains in loading state until the managed contest payload has been applied to local form state; PoolMaster unit coverage passes.
notes: Detected from GitHub Actions run 24971286701, job poolmaster-unit-tests. Failing test: CreateContestPage still allows category-picks configuration to be viewed from the manage flow.
close_reason: Fixed by keeping edit-mode contest setup in the loading state until the managed contest payload has hydrated local form state. Verified with targeted contest coverage and full PoolMaster unit coverage.

title: Mock provider registers unsupported Tennis and NCAA sync loops by default
description: QA core-api registers MockContestFeedAdapter for every sport declared by adapter.sportsCovered. The adapter declares GOLF, TENNIS, and NCAA_BASKETBALL even though QA sync is currently configured only for the Golf relative dataset. Scheduler startup then runs schedule, participant, ranking, live-score, and results loops for Tennis/NCAA and logs no-data warnings. Expected: scheduled sync only runs for sports explicitly configured for the active provider; Tennis/NCAA should not be registered or polled until provider binding/config enables them.
close_reason: Fixed by adding scheduledSports to ingestion config, reconciling scheduled loops from that config, filtering provider dashboard stats/unmapped participants to configured sports, and rejecting manual sync for unconfigured sports.

title: Ingestion ignores provider release and field-lock timestamps when no timing policy applies
description: Mock contest feed event details include schedule.releaseAt and schedule.fieldLocksAt, and the adapter stores them in SportEvent.metadata, but IngestionPersistence.resolveEventTiming only applies contest_timing_policies or falls back to startDate. With no seeded/default timing policies, imported QA relative events persist releaseAt/fieldLocksAt as startDate instead of the provider-provided relative times. Expected: provider timing metadata is honored when present, or equivalent timing policies are seeded so generated relative QA events produce the intended contest-eligible / locked / pending windows.
close_reason: Fixed by honoring provider metadata.releaseAt and metadata.fieldLocksAt in resolveEventTiming before falling back to timing policies/start date; covered by unit test.

title: Scheduled ingestion jobs are logged but not persisted to dashboard history
description: Scheduled ingestion jobs call IngestionScheduler.onJobComplete, but the app callback in packages/core-api/src/index.ts only logs the job and does not write an ingestion_jobs row. ProviderService.getIngestionDashboard reads ingestion_jobs for active/completed/failed job stats, so the root-admin sync dashboard can show all-zero stats and unavailable latest start even when CloudWatch proves scheduled jobs are running. Expected: scheduled job completions are persisted to ingestion_jobs or dashboard history reads the authoritative scheduled-job store.
close_reason: Fixed by persisting scheduler IngestionJobRecord completions to ingestion_jobs from the core-api onJobComplete callback; dashboard history now has scheduled job rows. Covered by persistence integration test, though local integration execution is blocked by Prisma P1001 while psql/functional API can reach Postgres.

title: Scheduled event sync polls stale event ids from inactive providers
description: QA scheduled live-score sync after successful deploy selected both golf-relative-live-now and stale event id 401811943. The active configured GOLF provider is mock-contest-feed, but the scheduled event reader queries sport_events by sport/status only and does not constrain provider_id. Result: mock-contest-feed is asked to poll live scores for a stale event owned by a different provider, completing with recordsProcessed=0 every 30 seconds and adding misleading sync-history/log noise. Expected: scheduled event feed candidates are scoped to the active provider for that sport, or skipped loudly when no active provider exists.
notes: QA confirmation after CI run 24969905812 / commit 8a4b826 deployed: new core-api task ecs/core-api/873c4c4fec9342c08627b66ea3df64a5 lists only golf-relative-live-now for GOLF EVENTLIVESCORES. Stale 401811943 events only came from the old draining task 7eaadc4beea2456cb366b1af6881572c and stopped after ECS service reached steady state. No core-api ERROR logs or FAILED ingestion jobs after steady state; latest GOLF live score job processed 80 records.
close_reason: Confirmed fixed in QA after commit 8a4b826 deploy: active core-api task scopes GOLF live-score candidates to mock-contest-feed event golf-relative-live-now only; stale 401811943 entries were limited to the old draining task and stopped after steady state.

title: Root-admin manage shell depends on league navigation and league list queries
description: Root-admin /manage and /manage/sync pages render the league selector and run the app-shell listLeagues query even though manage surfaces are platform administration, not league membership. Client logs during QA showed repeated appShell.leagues.failed entries on /manage/* with TypeError: Failed to fetch, adding noise to sync triage and coupling admin pages to member league state. Expected: /manage/* hides league selector/member nav and does not fetch leagues. Actual: root-admin manage pages still depend on league navigation state.
notes: Remediated locally: AppShell now skips league list query, league selector, league nav, and create-league modal on /manage routes so root-admin manage pages no longer depend on league membership shell state. Regression coverage added in app-shell test. Awaiting CI/deploy and QA retest.
close_reason: Closed as no longer valid after triage. AppShell now computes shouldLoadLeagueShell as authenticated AND not root-admin AND not /manage, so /manage/* hides the league selector/member nav/create-league shell and does not call listLeagues. Regression coverage exists in clients/poolmaster/src/features/app-shell/app-shell.test.tsx for /manage/sync and root-admin root redirect. Evidence: clients/poolmaster/src/features/app-shell/app-shell.tsx.

title: Manual event sync silently succeeds with zero records for unknown provider event ids
description: Manual event sync accepted eventId masters-2026 on 2026-04-26T21:44:26Z and returned 202/submitted, but the mock provider event id is golf-masters-2026. The resulting provider_sync_run completed with recordsProcessed=0 and no warning/error. Expected: invalid provider event ids fail fast with a visible not-found/error state or the UI offers selectable valid event ids. Actual: root-admin gets a successful-looking sync that imported no participants.
notes: Remediated locally: event participant sync now fails the sync run when provider.getEventDetails(eventId) returns null instead of completing with zero records; run-event-sync form now prompts with golf-masters-2026 provider id shape. Regression coverage added in ingestion scheduler and root-admin event-sync page tests. Awaiting CI/deploy and QA retest.
close_reason: Closed as no longer valid after triage. Manual event sync no longer depends on free-typed provider event IDs in the root-admin UI: Run Event Sync loads actual events for the selected sport, filters valid statuses by preset, requires selecting a loaded event, and submits that event externalId. Backend event participant sync also fails the run when provider.getEventDetails(eventId) returns null instead of completing with zero records. Evidence: clients/poolmaster/src/features/root-admin/root-admin-run-event-sync-page.tsx/test and packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/core/ingestion-scheduler.ts.

title: Live-score ingestion treats provider external participant ids as UUID participant ids
description: QA live-score sync is failing every 30 seconds for generated mock golf event golf-relative-live-now. CloudWatch shows Prisma P2023 on RosterPick: invalid UUID character 'g' because StatEventConsumer falls back from missing participantId to participantExternalId and ContestLookup queries sportEventParticipant.participantId with provider external ids like golfer-01. Expected: provider stat events resolve roster picks through provider participant mappings/source data without UUID parsing errors. Actual: EVENT_LIVE_SCORES_SYNC fails repeatedly, blocking live-score workflow signal.
notes: QA confirmation after latest deploy: GOLF live score scheduled sync now completes on active task with eventExternalId golf-relative-live-now, recordsProcessed=80, errors=0. No P2023/UUID errors or FAILED ingestion jobs after ECS steady state. Remaining stale 401811943 entries were from the old draining task during rollout, not the active task.
close_reason: Confirmed fixed in QA after commit 8a4b826 deploy: active core-api task processes golf-relative-live-now live-score sync with recordsProcessed=80/errors=0 and no P2023/UUID errors after ECS steady state.

title: Frontend: build unified Contest Board, delete MyEntriesPage, thin League Home contest list
description:
Frontend slice for pool-master-dxd.13. Fran-led. BLOCKED until pool-master-dxd.13.1 (Brad) lands and the SDK is regenerated.

PREREQ FROM BACKEND SLICE
=========================
Wait for the regenerated hey-api SDK to expose:
- listContestEntries response with top-level picksRevealed: boolean and entries[].picksCount: number; participants[] only present when picksRevealed === true.
- getContestEntry returning the same redacted shape when pre-event-start and requester is not the owning squad.

DO NOT START FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION BEFORE THESE TYPES EXIST. Per personas/fran.md §What This Persona Must Not Do.

title: Backend: enrich listEntries with participants when started; redact getEntry detail pre-start for non-owners
description:
Backend slice for pool-master-dxd.13. Brad-led.

EVENT-START PREDICATE
====================
'Pre-event-start' means contest.status === 'OPEN'.
'Event has started' means contest.status in ('IN_PROGRESS', 'COMPLETED', 'CANCELLED').
Reuse this predicate in both endpoints below; do not invent a parallel one.

ENDPOINT CHANGES
================
1. GET /api/v1/contests/:contestId/entries (existing operation listContestEntries)
   - When event has started: each entry includes a participants[] field with the same shape getEntryDetail returns today (rosterPicks → participantId, participantName, status, position, teamAffiliation, contestPoints, latestPerformance).
   - When pre-event-start: entries OMIT participants[]. Each entry includes a numeric picksCount (count of rosterPicks already made for this entry) so the leaderboard can display 'N picks made' without revealing which.
   - Response carries a top-level picksRevealed: boolean reflecting the predicate result, so the frontend does not reinvent the rule.

2. GET /api/v1/contests/:contestId/entries/:entryId (existing operation getContestEntry)
   - When event has started: full detail unchanged.
   - When pre-event-start AND requester's squad !== entry.squadId: return the redacted shape (no participants[]; picksRevealed:false; picksCount included). Do NOT 403 — the caller can still see the entry exists.
   - When pre-event-start AND requester is the owning squad (or has commissioner/root-admin authority over the entry's squad): full detail.

DTO / OPENAPI / MAPPER
======================
- ContestEntryDtoSchema: add picksCount (z.number().int().min(0)).
- ContestEntryListResponse and ContestEntryDetailResponse: add picksRevealed: boolean at the response level.
- ContestEntryDetailDtoSchema: keep participants[] but make it optional/nullable when picksRevealed is false. (Service-side: simply omit the field; client tolerates absence.)
- Update OpenAPI annotations and regenerate hey-api SDK.
- packages/core-api/src/modules/contests/service.ts loadEntryDtos and getEntryDetail: branch on the predicate, take requester squadId into account for getEntryDetail.
- handler.ts: pass requester userId/squadId through to service.getEntryDetail (currently it doesn't; that's part of this slice).

TESTS REQUIRED (per rules/testing-rules.md §3 Defect Verification)
==================================================================
Failing-first regression covering the visibility gap on getEntry:
- tests/functional/contests.functional.ts: case where contest.status === 'OPEN' and a non-owner squad member calls GET /contests/:contestId/entries/:entryId targeting another squad's entry. Today: returns rosterPicks. Must FAIL on broken code (asserting picks are absent), then PASS after fix.

Additional coverage:
- listEntries with status === 'OPEN': response.picksRevealed === false; entries[].participants is undefined; entries[].picksCount matches rosterPick count.
- listEntries with status === 'IN_PROGRESS' (or 'COMPLETED'): response.picksRevealed === true; entries[].participants populated for every entry.
- getEntry with status === 'OPEN' + owner: full participants[] returned.
- getEntry with status === 'OPEN' + non-owner: participants undefined; picksCount populated.
- getEntry with status === 'IN_PROGRESS' + non-owner: full participants[] returned.

QUALITY GATES (per AGENTS.md)
=============================
- npx turbo typecheck --force
- npx eslint ... --max-warnings 0
- npx jest --config tests/jest.config.js --forceExit
- npm run test:service:functional-api
- Regenerate SDK and confirm webapp typecheck still passes against the regenerated types.

DELIVERABLES
============
- Code changes under packages/core-api/src/modules/contests, packages/shared/dto/contests.dto.ts.
- OpenAPI doc updates.
- Regenerated packages/shared/generated/hey-api SDK.
- Failing-first defect test plus the four additional coverage cases above.
- Bead note recording the test results and gate outcomes before closing.
close_reason:

- getContestEntry — response gains picksRevealed: boolean. participants[] omitted when contest.status is DRAFT or OPEN AND requester is not the owning squad. Otherwise unchanged.

title: Consolidate contest views into one Contest Board; resolves N+1 fan-out and adds pre-live entry visibility rule
description:
## Summary
LeagueDetailPage fans out per-contest API calls in two parallel Promise.all loops:
- contestEntriesByContestQuery: listContestEntries({ contestId }) for every contest (clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/league-detail-page.tsx:209-237).
- contestDetailsByContestQuery: getContest({ contestId }) for every contest (:239-262).

For a league with N contests, this is 2N parallel HTTP requests every time the page mounts/refetches. With 50 contests that is 100 parallel requests for a single page load.

## Suggested approach
- Add a backend list endpoint that returns contest detail + entry counts in one call (preferred), or
- Paginate the visible contests on League Home and only fetch the visible page.

## Files
clients/poolmaster/src/features/leagues/league-detail-page.tsx:209-296
notes:
Design consolidation supersedes the original 'add a backend bundling endpoint' framing.

NEW SCOPE
=========
The original N+1 (LeagueDetailPage and MyEntriesPage each fanning out getContest + listContestEntries per contest) is resolved by a UX decomposition rather than a new bundling endpoint:

1. League Home and the league contest list are thin: they call only listContests and render contest cards. No per-contest fan-out.
2. MyEntriesPage is deleted. /league/:leagueCode/entries 301-equivalent redirects to /league/:leagueCode (matches the no-/my-leagues redirect pattern).
3. A single Contest Board view at /league/:leagueCode/contests/:contestId serves all of: pre-live entry list, post-live leaderboard, MY-only filter, and full LEAGUE view. The 'MY' vs 'LEAGUE' distinction is a client-side filter toggle on a single shared component.

CONTEST BOARD UX RULES
======================
- Header summary: contest name, status, 'My Entries: N · Total Entries: M'.
- Client-side toggle: 'My only' filter (does not refetch).
- Row spotlight (star/bold) when entry.squadId === myTeamId, regardless of filter state.
- Inline expand/collapse per row.
- When event has started: expanded row shows participants[] with elections + live data.
- When event is pre-start AND viewer is not the owning squad: expanded row shows 'Picks hidden until event starts · {picksCount} picks made'. The viewer's OWN entries always show full detail.
- Create-entry button visible when contest.status === 'OPEN', viewer's team is in the league, and entry limit not reached.

EVENT-START PREDICATE (confirmed)
=================================
'Event has started' = contest.status is anything other than 'OPEN' (i.e., IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, or CANCELLED).
'Pre-event-start' = contest.status === 'OPEN'.

VISIBILITY GAP DISCOVERED
=========================
Today's GET /api/v1/contests/:contestId/entries/:entryId (handler.ts:228-254, service.ts:238-295) returns ContestEntryDetailDto including rosterPicks WITHOUT any visibility check on contest status or requester squad. Any league member can see any other team's full pick list at any time. This is part of the backend slice (see child Brad bead) — must be tightened in the same release as the frontend consolidation, not after.

CHILD SLICES
============
- Backend (Brad) — enrich listEntries to include participants[] when post-event-start; redact getEntry when pre-event-start AND requester is not the owning squad. DTO/OpenAPI updates, mapper, contract tests, regenerated SDK.
- Frontend (Fran) — depends on Brad's regenerated SDK. Delete MyEntriesPage and add redirect; thin League Home/contest list to listContests only; build/repurpose Contest Board at /league/:leagueCode/contests/:contestId with the rules above.

This bead remains open until both child slices land and the visibility rule is verified end-to-end.
close_reason: Closed as no longer valid after triage. The backend child pool-master-dxd.13.1 and frontend child pool-master-dxd.13.2 are closed and the current code has the unified Contest Board, no LeagueDetailPage per-contest fan-out, enriched listContestEntries with picksRevealed/picksCount, and pre-live non-owner pick redaction. Remaining child pool-master-dxd.13.3 is a cleanup chore, not an open P1 defect.

title: Auth refresh guard re-arms whenever meQuery.error transitions, allowing repeated refresh attempts
description:
## Summary
auth-provider.tsx uses 'attemptedRefreshRef' to ensure /auth/refresh is only retried once after a /auth/me failure, but the ref is reset to false whenever 'meQuery.error' becomes falsy (clients/poolmaster/src/features/auth/auth-provider.tsx:82-168). A flapping me endpoint (intermittent 5xx) re-arms a fresh refresh on each error transition, producing repeated refresh attempts and noisy session churn.

## Effect
- Background tab with a flaky network can issue many refresh attempts.
- Each refresh failure clears local session state, bouncing the user to '/' even though the next attempt may have succeeded.

## Suggested approach
Gate refresh on the first error after mount only (or after a successful me-fetch resolves). Clear 'attemptedRefreshRef' explicitly on a successful me-refetch, not on every transition to a non-error state.

## Files
clients/poolmaster/src/features/auth/auth-provider.tsx:82-168
close_reason: Refresh guard now resets only inside the refresh-success path; flap-style me-query error transitions no longer re-arm refresh. Failing test added at src/features/auth/auth-provider.test.tsx ('does not re-fire refresh on a me-query flap once the initial refresh attempt has resolved'). Verified before/after fix; all AGENTS.md quality gates pass.

title: Vite dev proxy rewrites /api away, so every SDK call 404s against the backend
description:
## Summary
In dev, the webapp cannot reach any backend endpoint. The Vite proxy strips '/api' from the request path, but the generated hey-api SDK and the backend both speak '/api/v1/...'. The result: every API call (login, getCurrentUser, refreshToken, listLeagues, etc.) 404s, auth never hydrates, and the app appears as 'nothing loads'.

## Repro
1. Run 'npm run dev' from repo root (turbo runs core-api on :3000 and poolmaster on :5175).
2. Open http://localhost:5175.
3. Try to log in or register. Network tab shows requests to /api/v1/auth/* returning 404.

## Evidence
- clients/poolmaster/vite.config.ts:78-84 — proxy block: '{ target: \"http://localhost:3000\", rewrite: (p) => p.replace(/^\\/api/, \"\") }'.
- packages/shared/generated/hey-api/sdk.gen.ts — generated URLs include the /api/v1 prefix (e.g. url: '/api/v1/auth/register'). clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.test.ts:65 already asserts this.
- packages/core-api/src/index.ts:96-218 — backend mounts every module at '/api/v1/...'.

## Effect chain
client → /api/v1/auth/me → vite proxy strips /api → backend receives /v1/auth/me → 404 → auth-provider triggers refresh → also 404 → clearSessionState → user redirected to '/' permanently.

## Suggested fix
Remove the rewrite in clients/poolmaster/vite.config.ts so the proxy preserves the /api prefix:

  proxy: {
    '/api': {
      target: 'http://localhost:3000',
      changeOrigin: true,
    },
  },

## Verification protocol (per AGENTS.md §Defect Verification)
Add a failing test before the fix that asserts a request through the dev proxy reaches the backend with the /api/v1 prefix intact.

## Related
The new VITE_API_BASE_URL fallback (clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.ts:7-18) does NOT rescue dev because Vite reads .env* from clients/poolmaster/, not the repo root, and .env.example does not declare it.
notes:

Change set:
- clients/poolmaster/vite.config.ts: removed the 'rewrite: (p) => p.replace(/^\/api/, "")' line so the dev proxy preserves the /api prefix that both the generated SDK and the core-api backend already expect.
- clients/poolmaster/src/lib/dev-proxy.test.ts: NEW test referencing this defect ID. Asserts the config source has no /api-stripping rewrite and that the proxy targets http://localhost:3000.

Defect Verification Protocol (rules/testing-rules.md §3):
1. Failing-test step: ran 'npx vitest run src/lib/dev-proxy.test.ts' on the broken config — 1 test failed (stripsApiPrefix expected false, got true).
2. Apply-fix step: removed the rewrite line.
3. Passing-test step: re-ran same command — 2 tests passed.

Quality gates per AGENTS.md:
- npx turbo typecheck --force: 5/5 successful.
- npx eslint 'packages/*/src/**/*.ts' 'clients/*/src/**/*.{ts,tsx}' --max-warnings 0: clean.
- npx jest --config tests/jest.config.js --forceExit: 56 suites / 536 tests passed.
- npm run test:poolmaster:unit: 53 files / 155 tests passed.
- npm run test:service:functional-api: BLOCKED by missing poolmaster_test database (Prisma cannot connect). Pre-existing env requirement, not caused by this slice. Requires 'npm run db:test:reset' first per rules/testing-rules.md §functional-api.

Notes:
- Did not change the SDK or backend prefix; both keep using /api/v1 as the user explicitly requested.
- VITE_API_BASE_URL fallback in clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.ts stays in place but is no longer needed for default dev. Documenting it remains tracked under pool-master-dxd.22 / pool-master-dxd.23.
Functional API suite re-verified after clearing 11 stale tests/functional/server.ts processes (each holding Prisma connection pools, exhausting Postgres max_connections=100).

- Full suite (DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/poolmaster_test npm run test:service:functional-api):
  - 9/10 suites green: auth, leagues, squads, contests, client-logs, account, consent, root-admin, standings-history-consent.
  - drafts suite hit env-only failures (Too many database connections, FK violation in cleanup).
- After 'pkill -f tests/functional/server.ts' (active connections dropped from 98 to 1):

Confirms the proxy fix does not affect any backend contract surfaced through the functional suite. All AGENTS.md quality gates now pass.

title: Saving a new league icon breaks active league context and leaves the user unable to load any league
description: Manual testing blocker. While editing the MathWorks league and choosing a new icon, saving returned the user to a league-load failure state. The save also cleared the active MathWorks selection from the league selector. After that point, selecting any league produced a load failure, blocking further manual QA. Expected behavior: saving a league icon should persist the icon, preserve or restore the active league context, and allow the user to continue navigating leagues normally. Actual behavior: save appears to corrupt or invalidate league context, and the shell falls into 'We couldn't load your leagues' / 'We couldn't load this league' states across league selection. Evidence: 2026-04-25 manual QA screenshot showing the shell with 'We couldn't load your leagues.' after the icon-save attempt. Check browser/backend logs around the icon update request, active-league resolution, and post-save league-selector refresh.
notes:
Investigating client cache-refetch behavior; switching league mutations to authoritative cache writes to avoid follow-up GET/list failures.
Implemented cache-first league mutation updates and added regression coverage. Targeted league tests pass. Workspace typecheck/unit suite still show unrelated root-admin failures outside this slice.
Additional QA evidence on 2026-04-25: root-admin is also blocked sitewide. CloudWatch at 2026-04-25 11:12:40-11:15:02 UTC (7:12:40-7:15:02 AM EDT) shows rootAdminRoute.allowed on /manage, /manage/leagues, /manage/teams, and /manage/content-configuration for the root-admin user, but the correlated browser logs immediately emit appShell.leagues.failed with TypeError: Failed to fetch on those same routes. No corresponding admin manage success logs (for example adminLeagueService.search.success or adminListUsers-backed page loads) appear during that failure window, which indicates the root-admin outage is the same client-side fetch path already tracked in pool-master-dxd.7 rather than a separate backend authorization or route-specific defect.
Completed 2026-04-25. Replaced mutation-driven league query invalidations with authoritative league cache writes for create, detail edit/icon, manage edit/icon, and league removal flows so successful league mutations no longer trigger a follow-up shell/detail refetch. Strengthened the existing PoolMaster regression tests to prove the escaped scenario without synthetic failures: the create/manage/detail mutation paths now assert they sync existing queries without causing an extra fetch. Also fixed the root-admin manage scaffold typing break introduced during the badge-removal cleanup and codified defect-remediation workflow rules requiring real regression proof, no synthetic failures, and full local gate runs before closeout. Validation: npx turbo typecheck --force; npx eslint 'packages/*/src/**/*.ts' 'clients/*/src/**/*.{ts,tsx}' --max-warnings 0; npx jest --config tests/jest.config.js --forceExit; DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/poolmaster_test npm run test:service:functional-api; npm run test:poolmaster:unit; DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/poolmaster_test npm run test:coverage:service:merged.
Reopened on 2026-04-25 after QA found the same sitewide fetch blocker while opening Contest Home. CloudWatch for session 2224359b-bba3-4957-bb28-712ffe4cb6a9 / clientTraceId 5da09f85-0af5-4097-b1d3-fef3a6e3ba53 on release 130302d1f92290561b3ae5acd9430fb57160f54b shows memberRoute.allowed and appShell.loaded on /league/MATHWORKS/contests/312b7bb9-a417-48e2-b004-ec415e83b1d3, followed by contestDetail.contest.failed, leagueDetail.league.failed, and appShell.leagues.failed with TypeError: Failed to fetch. Additional log query for the same session found zero matching backend GET logs for /api/v1/leagues, /api/v1/leagues/MATHWORKS, /api/v1/leagues/league-1, or /api/v1/contests/312b7bb9-a417-48e2-b004-ec415e83b1d3 in that window, so the browser is failing before those requests reach core-api. Treat as a recurrence/expansion of the original frontend request-path blocker, not a separate backend authorization or data defect. Contest Home is currently a user-visible reproducer.
QA recurrence traced to app-side API base resolution. Added explicit VITE_API_BASE_URL support in clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.ts and regression coverage for base-url selection.
close_reason: Closed as no longer valid after triage. The active app now supports explicit VITE_API_BASE_URL resolution in clients/poolmaster/src/lib/api.ts, with regression coverage in api.test.ts, and league/icon mutation paths write authoritative cache updates rather than relying on fragile refetch behavior. title: Make root-admin sync workflow status truthful for async submissions
description: Manual testing defect. Root-admin provider sync is asynchronous, but the UI and sync history treated a 200 request/response as job success. Expected behavior: users only see workflow states like SUBMITTED, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, FAILED, or CANCELLED based on the actual sync run, and the admin UI shows the returned submission payload/details without claiming success before the async work finishes. Actual behavior: manual sync requests and sync history showed successful/completed states even when the underlying sync outcome was not successful.
notes: Implemented locally. Manual provider sync now returns 202 submission payloads and creates sync-run workflow records that advance through SUBMITTED, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, and FAILED based on the actual async execution. Root-admin UI no longer treats request acceptance as success, shows the response payload, and sync history exposes stored payload details instead of implying false completion.
close_reason: Closed as no longer valid after triage. Current root-admin manual sync routes return 202 submission payloads, create provider sync runs in SUBMITTED state, and later advance runs through IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED/FAILED via executeSubmittedFeedRun. The root-admin sync dashboard counts SUBMITTED separately and manual sync pages render the returned payload instead of claiming synchronous success. Evidence: packages/core-api/src/modules/admin/provider-handler.ts, provider-service.ts, clients/poolmaster/src/features/root-admin/*sync*.tsx, and contract verification coverage expecting 202 + SUBMITTED syncRuns.

title: Remove hidden service-tier provider fallback and fail loudly on missing sport provider config
description: Manual testing defect. Core API should never hide provider/configuration defects by falling back to built-in adapters. Expected behavior: service-tier ingestion uses only explicitly configured external provider services, and missing or wrong provider configuration fails loudly with ERROR/FATAL logging and a truthful error path. Actual behavior: QA silently used providerId="pga-tour" due to hidden fallback behavior, masking real deployment/config defects and producing misleading sync outcomes.
notes: Implemented locally. Removed service-tier fallback provider registration/selection from the active ingestion registry path, so only explicitly configured providers are available. Missing or unsupported provider configuration now logs at ERROR and leaves ingestion disabled instead of silently selecting built-in adapters. Updated provider registry tests and admin/provider service behavior accordingly.
close_reason: Closed as no longer valid after triage. Current provider binding startup has no hidden fallback: missing provider config logs ingestion.providers.unconfigured and leaves the registry empty; missing/unsupported configured providers log ERROR and throw. Regression coverage exists in tests/unit/core-api/provider-bindings.test.ts for unconfigured runtime registering no providers. Evidence: packages/core-api/src/modules/ingestion/core/provider-bindings.ts.

title: Adopt User Profile details/action layout for League and Team Details
description: Product/UI story. Standardize details-oriented pages around the User Profile page paradigm: primary details/status tiles on the left and a right-side action menu for lower-frequency actions. User Profile is the reference layout and should remain the pattern source. Apply this to League Details and Team Details so users see the same details/action structure across account, league, and team surfaces. League Details requirements: keep a League Details tile on the left; remove implementation note text that says 'League Home now owns the commissioner-editable identity fields directly instead of sending commissioners to a separate modal.' Move league status into the League Details tile, matching the User Profile details/status pattern. Replace the current inline League Invitations tile with a right-side action menu item named Invite Members. Invite Members opens a modal that contains the invitation flow; modal title/tile label becomes 'Invite Members'; replace existing invite helper text 'Commissioners and root admins can reuse the live invite flows directly from League Home.' with 'Invite new members to join the <league name> league'. League lifecycle actions should follow the User Profile convention: show Inactivate League when active; show Activate League and Delete League according to the existing lifecycle/delete rules when inactive; delete must keep the existing confirmation protections. Add a Leave League action button that opens a modal explaining the consequence, with Confirm and Cancel actions. After leave completes, show confirmation and an OK action. OK must move the user to another league from their league list when one exists; if no leagues remain, sign the user out. Team Details requirements: reorganize Team Details/Team Home to use the same left details/status tile plus right action-menu paradigm as User Profile and League Details. The Team Details tile is read-only: remove 'You can update your team name and choose a built-in icon here.'; show the team icon next to the read-only team name; show team status in the details tile; show team owners in the detail summary with each owner on their own row as <name>. Remove the Active Entry Management tile entirely because it duplicates navigation that belongs in the top menu. Move editable/less frequent work into the action menu. Team action menu items: Change Team Name, Change Team Icon, Manage Owners, Inactivate Team when active, and Delete Team conditionally when existing lifecycle/delete rules allow it. Preserve existing team lifecycle/delete rules, owner permissions, icon behavior, and any inactive/delete follow-up rules already defined. Acceptance: League Details, Team Details, and User Profile share the same page structure; details/status are readable on the left; less frequent actions live in the right action menu; Team Details is read-only and shows icon/name/status/owners; Active Entry Management tile is gone; Team edit/lifecycle/delete actions are modal/action-menu driven and conditionally available; League Invitations are modal-driven via Invite Members; lifecycle/leave/delete flows are modal-driven and permission-aware; no implementation-note copy is visible; tests cover commissioner/member/root-admin states and leave-league routing/logout behavior.
notes: Starting implementation batch. Sequence: first rework League Details and Team Details to the User Profile details/actions pattern, then replace the app shell button row with header dropdown navigation and split active contests/history.
close_reason: Implemented. App shell now uses header dropdown navigation with updated branding, icon-only notifications, no Help button, my-contests filter link, and split Active Contests / Contest History routes. title: Ingestion job failure ERROR log loses error message
description: Logging defect found during QA sync investigation. CloudWatch for the failed EVENT_PARTICIPANTS_SYNC at 2026-04-28T10:30:08.760Z logs an ERROR message 'Ingestion job failed' with fields jobType=EVENT_PARTICIPANTS_SYNC, providerId=mock-contest-feed, sport=GOLF, eventExternalId=golf-relative-manual-test-20260427t102759z, but the structured error field is serialized as an empty object: error={}. title: Send league invitation emails through a configured mail provider
description:
## User story
As a league commissioner, I want invite-by-email to send an actual email to each invited address so invitees can discover and accept the league invitation without needing the commissioner to manually copy/paste an invite code or link.

## Current behavior
The league invitation API creates pending email invitation records and returns them in the response, but the service does not call a mail provider. Manual QA observed that invite-by-email does not deliver an email. Code inspection confirms packages/core-api/src/modules/leagues/invitation-service.ts records InviteType.EMAIL invitations but has no SES/nodemailer/mail-delivery integration in that flow.

## Expected behavior
When invite-by-email succeeds, PoolMaster sends an invitation email containing the league name, inviter context where appropriate, accept URL/invite code, expiration context, and any commissioner message supported by the request.

## Suggested acceptance criteria
- Add a mail-delivery abstraction suitable for local/dev and deployed environments.
- Wire league email invitations to send after invitation records are created.
- Configure provider/runtime settings for QA/prod, likely SES or the platform mail provider chosen for PoolMaster.
- Log delivery attempts and failures without exposing sensitive email content.
- Decide whether API response should represent invitation-record creation, delivery submission, or both.
- Add tests for generated invitation content, provider invocation, and failure handling.

## Notes
This is a story, not a defect, because actual email delivery does not appear to be implemented yet.

title: Run Event Sync requires manual event id instead of valid loaded event selection
description:
## Summary
The root-admin Run Event Sync page currently requires typing an Event ID manually. That is not practical for manual QA or operations because root admins should not need to know provider/internal event IDs.

## Expected behavior
Replace the free-text Event ID entry with a dropdown/select populated from actual events already loaded into PoolMaster.

## Valid event rule
The selectable events should be valid for EVENT sync functionality: actual loaded events whose sport/feed/status make them eligible for event-level sync. The option label should show enough context to choose correctly, such as event name, sport, start date/time, lifecycle/status, provider/external ID if needed, and participant/score readiness state where available.

## Current evidence
clients/poolmaster/src/features/root-admin/root-admin-run-event-sync-page.tsx passes eventSyncEventId.trim() into adminSyncProviderEventData, and tests currently use typed IDs such as golf-masters-2026.

## Suggested implementation notes
- Use generated SDK/listEvents or add a backend/admin event-list endpoint if the existing event list lacks the fields/status needed for root-admin sync selection.
- Disable event-sync submission until a valid loaded event is selected.
- Keep sport/feed validation aligned with sync config rather than hardcoded sports/feed workarounds.
- Add frontend coverage proving free text is gone and only selectable loaded events can be submitted.
notes: Implemented Run Event Sync dropdown selection backed by listEvents instead of free-text Event ID. Event list contract now exposes provider externalId for event-level sync submission and includes OFFICIAL event status for result-ready events. UI filters selectable events by selected event-sync feed status, submits the provider externalId, and has regression coverage proving typed IDs are gone. Targeted verification passed: poolmaster page test, event service/handler unit test, api:refresh, api:validate. Targeted events integration test could not run because Prisma cannot connect to local Postgres from Node even though psql can connect; full gates/push deferred until batch completion.

title: Expose service and webapp version metadata for QA debugging
description:
## Summary
Add a first-class version metadata capability for debugging deployed QA/prod issues.

## Current state
- PoolMaster webapp builds already emit dist/version-info.json via clients/poolmaster/scripts/write-version-info.mjs.
- The webapp has getVersionInfo()/getEmbeddedVersionInfo() in clients/poolmaster/src/lib/version-info.ts.
- CI sets webapp/service SHA metadata during the webapp build.
- No core-api /version endpoint was found during intake.
- Existing webapp version-info.json does not clearly include generated client SDK/package version metadata.

## Requested enhancement
- Add a service-tier /version endpoint that returns API/service version info, git SHA, build number/build time, environment, and other operational metadata useful for debugging.
- Keep/extend webapp version-info.json with webapp version, git SHA, build number/time, asset release prefix, environment, client SDK/generated contract version if available, and the service version/SHA the webapp was built against.
- Document how to use both endpoints/artifacts when debugging failed-to-load or stale-deploy issues.

## Acceptance notes
- Endpoint should be unauthenticated if appropriate for diagnostics, but avoid exposing secrets or infrastructure internals.
- Response shapes should be typed/shared and covered by tests.
- Update README/docs for where to check deployed webapp metadata and service metadata.
notes: Implemented public service version endpoint at /version and /api/v1/version, webapp version-info client SDK/build-number metadata, CI task/webapp build stamping, generated OpenAPI/client updates, docs, and targeted unit coverage. Full gates and push are deferred until the requested batch is complete.

No production behavior changes expected; validation will be doc review plus link/path checks.
